items.q: Why was Tracey Emin's 'My Bed' taken seriously as art?
items.correct: Presented in a gallery as a confessional self-portrait, its concept, context and presentation carry the meaning.
items.misconception: Because it was secretly carved from marble.
items.random_wrong: Because beds are a traditional subject of painting.
items.why: Students look for hidden craft to justify conceptual works instead of engaging with idea, context and presentation.

items.q: Does postmodern irony and quotation mean 'anything goes' and nothing has meaning?
items.correct: No — appropriation, parody and pastiche are deliberate strategies commenting on culture and originality.
items.misconception: Yes — postmodernism rejects all meaning, so nothing can be analysed.
items.random_wrong: Yes — postmodern works are generated at random.
items.why: Students mistake the questioning of grand narratives for total meaninglessness, missing postmodernism's critical strategies.

items.correct: Young British Artists — a generation including Hirst and Emin who emerged around 1988-1997 with shows like Freeze and Sensation.
items.misconception: A Victorian society of academic painters.
items.random_wrong: A band famous for designing album covers.
items.why: Students misplace the YBAs historically; they emerged from Goldsmiths in the late 1980s and dominated the 1990s.

items.q: Sensation opened at the Royal Academy in:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1997. “1987” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Tracey Emin's My Bed dates from:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1998. “1988” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If Freeze opened in 1988 and Sensation in 1997, how many years between them?
items.why: The real figure is 9. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
